name: Carrot-And-Turnip Gratin
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Thinly sliced scallion greens (not bulb)
1/2 cup Heavy cream, half-and-half or additional milk
1 tbsp Cold unsalted butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a shallow 1 1/2-quart baking dish or a 10-inch round or oval gratin dish and set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, toss together carrots, turnips, scallion greens, parsley, and 3 tablespoons of cornstarch.
3. Spread the vegetable mixture in the prepared pan, pressing and smoothing it into place.
4. In a small saucepan, dissolve the remaining cornstarch in 1/4 cup of milk.
5. When completely dissolved, add cream and remaining milk. Bring to a boil over moderately high heat, whisking constantly.
6. Reduce to moderate heat. In a medium mixing bowl, beat egg with a fork and season with salt and pepper.
7. Add the egg to the milk mixture in slow, steady streams, beating until combined.
8. Pour this hot custard over the vegetable mixture in the gratin dish.
9. Sprinkle the top evenly with Parmesan cheese. (The gratin can be prepared up to this point and refrigerated.)
10. Dot the gratin with butter and bake in the middle of a preheated 375°F oven for 45 minutes, or until it is bubbling through a golden crust. Let the gratin stand 10 minutes before serving to crisp the crust and allow the custard to set.
